content:
Title
* Why specify the "knight"?

Goal
* Please change "lines of" to "rows and".

* Please change "free" to "empty".

* Please replace

Each piece is identified with a letter (upper case for one player, lower case for the other). You just have to write down the move with its coordinates.

with:

Each piece may appear on the board in uppercase (white) or lowercase (black).

Any character not in the set of valid piece letters (in either uppercase or lowercase) represents an empty cell. The character used for an empty cell may differ between the two board states.

Your task is to output the move with its coordinates.

Input
* Please rewrite this section as follows:

<<Line 1>>: A string [[pieces]] containing the uppercase English letters that represent the white pieces. (The corresponding lowercase letters represent the black pieces.)
<<Next 8 lines>>: The board before the move.
<<Next 8 lines>>: The board after the move.

Each line of the board is a string of 8 printable ASCII characters. Each character is either a piece or an empty cell.

Output
* Please add "<<Line 1>>:" before "Coordinates".

* Please change "was move" to "was moved".

* Please format "a", "h", "1", "8", "a8", "-" and "x" as {{constants}}.

* Please add ", from left to right" after "on the board".

* Please add ", from bottom to top" after "vertical position".

* Please remove the examples.

Constraints
* Please replace "free" with "empty".

* Please remove the entire "caution" sentence as this is not a constraint (and is no longer necessary after modifying the Goal section as described above).

Difficulty
* Consider changing the difficulty from Medium to Easy.

Cases
* Please remove the trailing newlines from the input of all the cases.

* Consider changing Crazy case (Test 4) such that the output is a simple move, similar to Validator 4. This would also ensure more balanced test coverage (50% simple moves and 50% captures).

* I'll review the cases again once the above changes have been applied.

Thanks.
